catalyst_summary: Cassava Sciences (SAVA) announced on November 25, 2024, that topline results from the Phase 3 ReThink-ALZ study of simufilam in mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's disease did not meet co-primary endpoints (ADAS-COG12 and ADCS-ADL) or secondary and biomarker endpoints after 52 weeks.[1][2][3]
pivotal_evidence: Phase 3 ReThink-ALZ study designed as a registrational trial for mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's disease.
primary_endpoint_result: Failed to meet co-primary endpoints (ADAS-COG12 and ADCS-ADL) after 52 weeks.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Parallel-group, 52-week Study Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of Simufilam 100 mg Tablets in Subjects With Mild-to-Moderate Alzheimer's Disease

catalyst_summary: BioAge Labs announced in December 2024 the discontinuation of the STRIDES Phase 2 clinical trial for azelaprag due to unexpected liver transaminitis observed in some patients, marking the key clinical data update around that time.[1][3][4]
primary_endpoint_result: Trial discontinued due to unexpected liver transaminitis in 11 of 204 patients on azelaprag; no efficacy data reported as primary endpoints of mean percent change in body weight at Week 24 not reached.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel-Arm Proof-of-Concept Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of Oral Azelaprag Plus Once Weekly Tirzepatide Compared with Tirzepatide Alone in Participants with Obesity Aged 55 Years and Over (STRIDES)

catalyst_summary: Cassava Sciences (SAVA) announced topline results from the Phase 3 RETHINK-ALZ trial for simufilam in Alzheimer's disease in November 2024, close to December 4, 2024, showing failure to meet primary endpoints but a favorable safety profile.[1][2][3]
pivotal_evidence: Phase 3 registrational trial evaluating safety and efficacy in mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's disease
primary_endpoint_result: Failed to meet co-primary endpoints of Alzheimer’s Disease Assessment Scale-Cognitive Subscale 12 and Alzheimer’s Disease Cooperative Study-Activities of Daily Living over 52 weeks.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Parallel-group, 52-week Study Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of Simufilam 100 mg Tablets in Subjects With Mild-to-Moderate Alzheimer's Disease

catalyst_summary: On November 18, 2025, the EHDN held a platform meeting discussing uniQure's topline results from the AMT-130 Phase I/II gene therapy trial for Huntington's disease, unpacking the outcomes following recent updates.[5]
pivotal_evidence: Company expected to submit biologics license application (BLA) in Q1 2026 based on these results, with FDA alignment on using cUHDRS as intermediate clinical endpoint for accelerated approval
primary_endpoint_result: High-dose AMT-130 achieved 75% slowing of disease progression on the composite Unified Huntington's Disease Rating Scale (cUHDRS) compared with external controls (P=0.003), meeting the primary endpoint at 36 months
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Sham Control and Open-Label Study to Explore Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy Signals of Multiple Doses of Striatally-Administered rAAV5-miHTT Total Huntingtin Gene (HTT) Lowering Therapy (AMT-130) in Early Manifest Huntington's Disease

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for ZNTL were announced on or around July 3, 2024; the closest updates were in Q2 2024 financials on August 9 and later in December 2024 for the DENALI trial showing ~35% ORR in Cyclin E1+ platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.

pivotal_evidence: DENALI trial Part 2 is registration-intent with topline data expected by end of 2026[2][5]

catalyst_summary: BioAge Labs (BIOA) announced on December 6, 2024, the discontinuation of its STRIDES Phase 2 clinical trial for azelaprag due to observed liver transaminitis in some patients, based on interim safety data from the ongoing study.[3][1][4]
primary_endpoint_result: Trial discontinued prior to primary completion due to liver transaminitis in 11 of 204 participants in azelaprag arms, with no efficacy data reported.

catalyst_summary: Fulcrum Therapeutics shared topline results for pociredir in September 2024, showing a mean increase of 8.6% in fetal hemoglobin and a 0.9 g/dL increase in total hemoglobin after 12 weeks of treatment in sickle cell disease patients.
primary_endpoint_result: At week 12, all participants achieved increases in fetal hemoglobin (HbF), with a mean increase of 8.6% in the 12 mg dose cohort, and 44% of patients achieving HbF levels above 20%, a threshold associated with reduced vaso-occlusive crises.
ct_official_title: An Open-Label Extension Study to Evaluate Long-Term Safety and Tolerability of Pociredir in Participants With Sickle Cell Disease (SCD) Who Have Participated in a Pociredir Study

catalyst_summary: Sangamo Therapeutics announced updates from the Phase 1/2 STAAR trial for ST-920 (isaralgagene civaparvovec) in Fabry disease around mid-September 2024, noting that all 18 ERT patients were withdrawn from ERT and maintained normal alpha-Gal A activity levels as of that date.

primary_endpoint_result: Met primary safety endpoint with excellent tolerability, no prophylactic steroids needed, and favorable profile allowing all ERT patients to discontinue therapy while maintaining benefits[1][2][3]

catalyst_summary: Alto Neuroscience announced topline results from the Phase 2b trial of ALTO-100 for major depressive disorder on October 22, 2024, which did not achieve the primary outcome but showed positive findings in pre-specified secondary analyses and biomarker-stratified patients.[5][6][2]
primary_endpoint_result: Did not meet primary endpoint of change from baseline in MADRS compared to placebo in biomarker-defined MDD patients.
ct_official_title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study Followed by Open-Label Treatment of ALTO-100 in Adults With Bipolar Disorder Currently Experiencing a Major Depressive Episode

catalyst_summary: Bicycle Therapeutics announced updated Phase 1/2 clinical results on September 16, 2024, for multiple oncology candidates including zelenectide pevedotin showing 45% ORR in metastatic urothelial cancer and BT7480 demonstrating a favorable safety profile in advanced solid tumors.
pivotal_evidence: The Phase 2/3 Duravelo-2 registrational trial is designed to evaluate zelenectide pevedotin plus pembrolizumab versus chemotherapy in first-line mUC, providing multiple opportunities for potential accelerated approval[1]
primary_endpoint_result: Updated Phase 1 combination data showed 60% overall response rate for zelenectide pevedotin plus pembrolizumab in first-line cisplatin-ineligible mUC patients, with a median duration of response of 11.1 months[4][5]
ct_official_title: A Randomized Open-Label Phase 2/3 Study of BT8009 as Monotherapy or in Combination in Participants With Locally Advanced or Metastatic Urothelial Cancer (Duravelo-2)

catalyst_summary: BioAge Labs (BIOA) announced on December 6, 2024, the discontinuation of its Phase 2 STRIDES trial for azelaprag due to elevated liver enzymes observed in 11 of 204 enrolled patients, with plans to analyze data and provide an update in Q1 2025.[1][2]
primary_endpoint_result: Trial was discontinued in December 2024 due to liver transaminitis observed in 11 of 204 patients treated with azelaprag, before primary efficacy endpoints at Week 24 could be assessed.

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for the MIST trial (NCT06329401) were announced on or around April 10, 2025; available information includes only the trial protocol published in December 2025 and Avalyn Pharma's general pulmonary fibrosis program presentations at ATS 2025 on May 6, 2025.
pivotal_evidence: Phase 2b randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled design (MIST study, NCT06329401) is a pivotal trial evaluating safety and efficacy of AP01 in progressive pulmonary fibrosis, positioned to support future regulatory approval
primary_endpoint_result: No clinical trial results for the MIST trial were announced on or around April 10, 2025; the trial was ongoing as of the search results, with long-term data from the Phase 1b ATLAS extension study (up to 240 weeks) showing continued antifibrotic effects and favorable safety profile
ct_official_title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Phase 2b Study Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of Pirfenidone Solution for Inhalation (AP01) in Participants With PPF

catalyst_summary: Verastem Oncology (VSTM) presented initial interim safety and efficacy results from the ongoing RAMP 205 trial of avutometinib and defactinib with gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel in first-line metastatic pancreatic cancer on June 1, 2024, at the ASCO Annual Meeting, close to June 12, 2024.[1]
pivotal_evidence: Registration-directed trial with NDA submitted under Accelerated Approval pathway based on primary ORR data[1][2][3]
primary_endpoint_result: Confirmed overall response rate (ORR) by blinded independent review in the primary analysis with June 30, 2024 cutoff[1]
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study of Combination Therapy With Avutometinib Plus Defactinib Versus Investigator's Choice of Treatment in Patients With Recurrent Low-Grade Serous Ovarian Cancer (LGSOC) (RAMP 301)

catalyst_summary: Multiple ATRA-based clinical trial results were announced in early 2024, including ATRA/ATO regimen data for high-risk APL presented at the 2024 EHA Congress showing 88% event-free survival at 2 years.

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for the MIST study (NCT06329401), a phase 2b trial of inhaled pirfenidone in progressive pulmonary fibrosis, were announced on or around 2025-04-08; only the trial protocol was published on 2025 Dec 25.[1]
primary_endpoint_result: No clinical trial results available; primary endpoint is change from baseline in forced vital capacity at week 52.
ct_official_title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Phase 2b Study Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of Pirfenidone Solution for Inhalation (AP01) in Participants With PPF

catalyst_summary: VYNE Therapeutics announced topline results from its Phase 2b trial of repibresib gel for vitiligo on August 14, 2025, which missed primary endpoints of F-VASI50 and F-VASI75 but showed nominally significant effects in secondary endpoints for the 3% concentration.
primary_endpoint_result: Failed to meet primary endpoints of proportion achieving >50% improvement (F-VASI50) and >75% improvement (F-VASI75) in facial vitiligo score.

catalyst_summary: Rezolute (RZLT) announced topline Phase 3 sunRIZE trial results for ersodetug in congenital hyperinsulinism on December 11, 2025, which missed primary and key secondary endpoints despite showing a 45% reduction in hypoglycemia events at the top dose compared to 40% placebo improvement (not statistically significant).[1][3]

primary_endpoint_result: Failed to meet primary endpoint of change in average weekly hypoglycemia events, with 45% reduction at top dose (10 mg/kg) versus 40% improvement in placebo arm, difference not statistically significant[3]

catalyst_summary: Alto Neuroscience (ANRO) announced topline results from the Phase 2b trial of ALTO-100 for major depressive disorder (MDD) in October 2024, reporting no statistically significant improvement in the primary endpoint for biomarker-positive patients compared to placebo, though favorable safety and subgroup signals were noted.[1][4]
primary_endpoint_result: ALTO-100 did not demonstrate statistically significant improvement in depressive symptoms compared to placebo in biomarker-positive patients, as measured by change from baseline in Montgomery-Åsberg Depression Rating Scale (MADRS).[2]

catalyst_summary: Sangamo Therapeutics presented detailed clinical data from the registrational STAAR study showing isaralgagene civaparvovec as a potential one-time, durable treatment for Fabry disease with positive eGFR slope results and durability of effect demonstrated for up to 4.5 years.
pivotal_evidence: FDA agreed that 52-week mean annualized eGFR slope can serve as primary basis for approval under Accelerated Approval pathway; company initiated rolling BLA submission in December 2025[1][3]

ct_official_title: A Phase I/II, Multicenter, Open-Label, Single-Dose, Dose-Ranging Study to Assess the Safety and Tolerability of ST-920, an AAV2/6 Human Alpha Galactosidase A Gene Therapy, in Subjects With Fabry Disease (STAAR)

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for SGMO were announced on or around 2024-08-22; the closest updates were Q2 2024 results on August 15 mentioning Fabry disease progress and mid-September 2024 data on sustained positive results in the STAAR trial.

primary_endpoint_result: Positive mean annualized estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) slope at 52 weeks across all dosed patients, with stable cardiac function over one year and durability of alpha-galactosidase A activity maintained for up to 4.5 years[2]

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results were announced by RAPT on or around 2024-02-28; instead, the FDA placed clinical holds on the Phase 2 asthma and atopic dermatitis trials of zelnecirnon due to a single serious adverse event of severe liver injury.[1][2][3][4][5]
primary_endpoint_result: Trials were placed on clinical hold by FDA due to severe liver injury SAE and closed before completing enrollment, with no endpoint results announced.

catalyst_summary: Keros Therapeutics announced top-line results from the TROPOS Phase 2 trial of cibotercept in PAH on or around December 2024, including halting higher dose arms on December 12, 2024 due to pericardial effusions and full trial termination on January 15, 2025, ultimately discontinuing development in PAH.[1]

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for GRAL (Galleri) were announced on or around March 17, 2025; PATHFINDER 2 results were announced on October 17, 2025.

primary_endpoint_result: Trial discontinued due to two severe Grade 3 unconjugated bilirubin elevations in the multiple-ascending dose portion, which were reversible without liver injury.

catalyst_summary: Rezolute announced Phase 3 sunRIZE trial results on December 11, 2025, showing the drug failed to meet its primary and key secondary endpoints for congenital hyperinsulinism, though it demonstrated a 45% reduction in hypoglycemia events compared to 40% in placebo.

catalyst_summary: AnaptysBio (ANAB) announced on or around December 19, 2024, that its Phase 2 trial of rosnilimab in moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis did not meet the Week 12 primary endpoint or key secondary endpoints, leading to discontinuation of the program.[1]

catalyst_summary: Sutro Biopharma announced expanded clinical trial data from the dose-optimization portion of the REFRαME-O1 trial with luvelta (STRO-002) in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer patients at the SGO 2025 Annual Meeting on March 15, 2025, showing consistent response rates across FRα expression levels of 25% or greater.

catalyst_summary: Ironwood announced FDA approval of apraglutide for short bowel syndrome with intestinal failure on April 14, 2025, based on positive Phase 3 STARS trial data showing significant reductions in weekly parenteral support volume and achievement of enteral autonomy in patients.
indication: short bowel syndrome with intestinal failure
pivotal_evidence: STARS trial is described as the pivotal Phase 3 trial, largest SBS-IF trial to date, with plans for NDA submission despite needing confirmatory trial[1][2][4]
primary_endpoint_result: Generated strong safety and efficacy data in STARS Phase 3, with significant reductions in parenteral support volume and more patients achieving enteral autonomy[1][2]

catalyst_summary: Sensei Biotherapeutics announced preliminary safety and efficacy data in March 2025 highlighting favorable activity in PD-(L)1 resistant tumors, with full dose expansion data including 6-month progression-free survival results presented at ESMO Congress in October 2025.
indication: advanced solid tumors resistant to prior PD-(L)1 therapy
primary_endpoint_result: Preliminary data showed promising safety and efficacy with 14% ORR and 62% disease control rate in 21 patients with 'hot' tumors, and 50% six-month progression-free survival in the 15-mg/kg cohort.

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for SGMO were announced on or around January 6, 2025; detailed STAAR study data for isaralgagene civaparvovec in Fabry disease was presented later at WORLDSymposium 2026 with prior topline results in June 2025 and BLA initiation in November-December 2025.
pivotal_evidence: FDA agreed that eGFR slope at 52 weeks from the Phase 1/2 STAAR study can serve as the primary basis for Accelerated Approval, eliminating the need for an additional registrational study[1][2]
primary_endpoint_result: The STAAR study demonstrated a positive mean annualized eGFR slope at 52 weeks across all dosed patients, with statistically significant improvements in both mean and median eGFR levels in the 18 patients with more than one year of follow-up data[1][2]

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for SMMT were announced on or around April 30, 2025; the closest announcements were HARMONi-6 topline PFS results on April 23, 2025, and HARMONi topline results in May 2025.
indication: EGFR-mutated, locally advanced or metastatic non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) after third-generation EGFR TKI failure
pivotal_evidence: HARMONi is a multiregional Phase III trial sponsored by Summit evaluating ivonescimab for potential global regulatory approval outside China[1][2][6]
primary_endpoint_result: Topline PFS results announced in May 2025; 45% risk reduction in China patients (statistically significant) but only 33% in Western patients (not statistically significant)[2][5]

catalyst_summary: Invivyd (IVVD) announced positive Phase 1/2 clinical trial data for VYD2311, a monoclonal antibody for COVID-19 prevention, on February 3, 2025, showing favorable safety, pharmacokinetics, and antiviral activity, but no announcements occurred on or around February 19, 2025.[1]
primary_endpoint_result: No specific results reported; Phase 1 data readout on potency, half-life, and safety anticipated later in Q2 2025, with primary endpoint of RT-PCR-confirmed symptomatic COVID-19 through Day 90.

catalyst_summary: VYNE Therapeutics announced topline results from its Phase 2b trial of repibresib gel in nonsegmental vitiligo on July 30, 2025, which missed primary and key secondary endpoints but showed some positive signals in secondary measures at the highest dose; the company terminated the trial extension.[1][2]
primary_endpoint_result: Failed to meet primary endpoint of proportion of subjects achieving >50% improvement in facial vitiligo score and key secondary endpoint of >75% improvement.
No data released around 2024-03-14; MIST trial initiated September 2024 with primary endpoint of change from baseline in FVC at Week 52.
ct_official_title: A Multinational, Long-Term, Safety and Tolerability, Open-Label Extension Study of Subjects Who Have Participated in Avalyn Pharma Studies of Inhaled Antifibrotic Agents (AP-LTE-008 [SAIL])
ct_conditions: Progressive Pulmonary Fibrosis; Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF)

catalyst_summary: Kala Bio announced topline results from the CHASE Phase 2b trial of KPI-012 for persistent corneal epithelial defect (PCED) around late September 2025, reporting that it missed the primary endpoint and key secondary endpoints, leading to discontinuation of development.[2][5]
pivotal_evidence: Company stated CHASE Phase 2b could serve as first of two pivotal trials for BLA submission if positive[1][5]
primary_endpoint_result: KPI-012 failed to meet the primary endpoint and key secondary endpoints in the CHASE Phase 2b trial for PCED[3]
ct_official_title: A Study to Evaluate Safety and Efficacy of KPI-012 Ophthalmic Solution in Participants With Persistent Corneal Epithelial Defect (PCED)

catalyst_summary: Lexeo Therapeutics announced positive interim Phase 1/2 clinical trial data for LX2006 on July 15, 2024, showing reductions in left ventricular mass index and other cardiac improvements, with related news coverage on July 18, 2024.
primary_endpoint_result: Interim data showed mean reduction in left ventricular mass index of 11.4% at 12 months and 18.3% at 18 months in participants with elevated baseline levels, with improvements in other cardiac biomarkers and increased frataxin expression.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2 Study of the Safety and Efficacy of LX2006 Gene Therapy in Participants With Cardiomyopathy Associated With Friedreich's Ataxia

catalyst_summary: Invivyd (IVVD) announced positive 180-day clinical efficacy data from the CANOPY Phase 3 trial of pemivibart on August 27, 2024, showing an 84% relative risk reduction in symptomatic COVID-19 compared to placebo in immunocompetent individuals and a 3% rate in immunocompromised patients.[1]
pivotal_evidence: Phase 3 randomized placebo-controlled trial supporting EUA for COVID-19 prevention
primary_endpoint_result: Met primary endpoint with 84% relative risk reduction in symptomatic COVID-19 vs placebo through month 6 in immunocompetent participants.
ct_official_title: A Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of VYD222 for Prevention of COVID-19 (CANOPY)

catalyst_summary: Sangamo reported detailed Phase 1/2 STAAR study data showing positive kidney function results with a mean annualized eGFR slope of 1.965 mL/min/1.73m²/year at 52 weeks and sustained α-Gal A activity up to 4.5 years in Fabry disease patients.
pivotal_evidence: FDA agreed to use eGFR slope from this registrational Phase 1/2 STAAR study as primary basis for accelerated approval, avoiding need for additional registrational study[1][3][4][6]
primary_endpoint_result: Positive mean annualized eGFR slope of 1.965 mL/min/1.73m²/year at 52 weeks across all dosed patients, improving upon typical decline in Fabry disease[1][3][5]
ct_official_title: A Phase I/II, Multicenter, Open-Label, Single-Dose, Dose-Ranging Study to Assess the Safety and Tolerability of ST-920, an AAV2/6 Human Alpha Galactosidase A Gene Therapy, in Subjects With Fabry Disease (STAAR)

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for SAVA (Cassava Sciences) were announced on or around April 4, 2025; the closest was topline Phase 3 results for simufilam in Alzheimer's disease reported on March 25, 2025, confirming failure to meet endpoints, with peer-reviewed publication in January 2026.
pivotal_evidence: Phase III ReThink-ALZ study (NCT04994483) was a registrational trial designed to support FDA approval in mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's disease.
primary_endpoint_result: Failed to meet co-primary endpoints of change in cognition (ADAS-COG12) and function (ADCS-ADL) after one year, along with all secondary and exploratory biomarker endpoints.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Parallel-group, 52-week Study Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of Simufilam 100 mg Tablets in Subjects With Mild-to-Moderate Alzheimer's Disease

catalyst_summary: Verrica Pharmaceuticals (VRCA) announced positive preliminary topline results from Part 2 of its Phase 2 clinical trial of VP-315 for basal cell carcinoma on August 14, 2024, showing 51% complete histological clearance, 86% overall tumor size reduction, and good tolerability with no serious adverse events.
primary_endpoint_result: Achieved 51% complete histological clearance and 86% overall tumor size reduction in Part 2 of the Phase 2 trial.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2, Multicenter, Open-label, Proof-of-concept Study With Safety Run-in to Evaluate the Safety, Pharmacokinetics, and Efficacy of VP-315 in Adult Subjects With Basal Cell Carcinoma
ct_conditions: Basal Cell Carcinoma; Skin Cancer; Cancer of the Skin, Basal Cell; Cancer of the Skin; Carcinoma

catalyst_summary: Sana Biotechnology announced positive initial clinical results on January 7, 2025, from a first-in-human type 1 diabetes study of UP421 islet cell transplantation without immunosuppression, showing cell survival and insulin production at 4 and 12 weeks in one patient.
primary_endpoint_result: Met primary safety endpoint with no safety issues observed and evidence of cell survival at multiple timepoints including six months post-transplantation.
ct_official_title: First-in-human Safety Study of Hypoimmune Pancreatic Islet Transplantation in Adult Subjects With Type 1 Diabetes

catalyst_summary: ImmunityBio (IBRX) announced interim clinical trial data from QUILT-106 on November 12, 2024, showing 100% disease control and durable complete responses (up to 15 months) in 4 Waldenstrom lymphoma patients treated with chemo-free CD19 CAR-NK plus rituximab.[1][7]
primary_endpoint_result: Interim data showed 100% disease control with durable complete responses up to 15 months in 4 Waldenstrom lymphoma patients treated with CD19 CAR-NK plus rituximab[1][7].
ct_official_title: An Exploratory Clinical Study of the Safety and Efficacy of CD19 Chimeric Antigen Receptor NK Cell Injection for the Treatment of Relapsed/Refractory Autoimmune Diseases

catalyst_summary: ORIC Pharmaceuticals reported Phase 1b clinical trial data for ORIC-944 in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) on February 18, 2025, including encouraging early safety and efficacy results from ongoing dose escalation trials in combination with androgen receptor inhibitors like apalutamide and darolutamide.[4]
primary_endpoint_result: Determined recommended Phase 2 dose (RP2D) via 3+3 dose escalation design with encouraging PSA50 response rate of 55% (40% confirmed) and ctDNA reductions in 76% of patients.
ct_official_title: An Open-Label, Phase 1/1b Study of ORIC-944 as a Single Agent or in Combination With an Androgen Receptor Pathway Inhibitor in Patients With Metastatic Prostate Cancer

catalyst_summary: Alto Neuroscience (ANRO) announced topline results from the Phase 2b trial of ALTO-100 for major depressive disorder (MDD) on October 22, 2024, which failed to meet its primary endpoint of change in MADRS score compared to placebo.[8][9]
primary_endpoint_result: Failed to meet primary endpoint of change in MADRS score compared to placebo.
ct_official_title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study Followed by Open-Label Treatment of ALTO-100 in Adults With Bipolar Disorder Currently Experiencing a Major Depressive Episode
ct_conditions: Bipolar Disorder I or II With a Major Depressive Episode

catalyst_summary: MacroGenics (MGNX) announced interim updated safety and efficacy data from the TAMARACK study on May 9, 2024 (data cutoff April 12, 2024), reporting five patient deaths including three potentially related to vobra duo, with stock crashing reported around May 29, 2024; no separate clinical trial results were announced precisely on May 29, 2024.
pivotal_evidence: Phase 2 randomized trial designed to evaluate efficacy and safety with primary endpoint of landmark 6-month radiographic progression-free survival, intended to support regulatory advancement
primary_endpoint_result: Landmark 6-month radiographic PFS of 69% and 70% for the 2.0mg/kg and 2.7mg/kg doses respectively, numerically above docetaxel's ~8 months median rPFS; however, efficacy was offset by safety concerns with eight patient deaths reported, leading MacroGenics to discontinue further internal development in March 2025
ct_official_title: A Phase 1, First-in-Human, Dose Escalation Study of MGD024, a CD123 x CD3 Bispecific DART Molecule, in Patients With Select Relapsed or Refractory Hematologic Malignancies
ct_conditions: Leukemia, Acute Myeloid; Myelodysplastic Syndromes; Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma; Leukemia, B-cell; Leukemia, Hairy Cell; Mastocytosis, Aggressive Systemic; Blastic Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cell Neoplasm; Chronic Myeloid Leukemia

catalyst_summary: Omeros (OMER) reported third-quarter 2025 results on November 13, 2025, including key clinical data for narsoplimab showing 61% complete response (CR) in the pivotal TA-TMA study (N=28), 68% CR in evaluable expanded access patients, and 73-74% 100-day survival rates.[1][4]
indication: hematopoietic stem cell transplant-associated thrombotic microangiopathy (TA-TMA)
pivotal_evidence: Completed pivotal Phase 3 trial with significant survival benefit compared to external controls; BLA resubmitted to FDA in March 2025 with PDUFA date in late September 2025
primary_endpoint_result: Pivotal Phase 3 trial demonstrated 61% complete response rate in the primary study population (N=28), 68% complete response in evaluable expanded access patients, and 73-74% 100-day survival rates, showing significant survival benefit compared to external controls
ct_official_title: I-SPY COVID TRIAL: An Adaptive Platform Trial to Reduce Mortality and Ventilator Requirements for Critically Ill Patients

catalyst_summary: Verastem Oncology (VSTM) announced positive initial interim safety and efficacy results from the RAMP 205 Phase 1/2 trial on May 23, 2024, with 83% (5/6) confirmed partial responses in cohort 1, presented at ASCO on June 1, 2024, and updated results announced around June 2, 2024.[1][2]
primary_endpoint_result: Initial interim results showed 83% (5/6) confirmed partial responses (ORR) in cohort 1 receiving avutometinib plus defactinib with gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2 Trial of the Doublet Combination of Avutometinib and Defactinib and as a Triplet in Combination With Temozolomide in Patients With High Grade Malignant Brain Tumours Within the 5G Platform
ct_conditions: Glioblastoma Multiforme (GBM); Glioblastoma Multiform (Grade IV Astrocytoma); Diffuse Hemispheric Glioma, H3 G34-Mutant; Malignant Primary Gliomas
ct_sponsor: Institute of Cancer Research, United Kingdom

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results were announced for NTLA on or around October 31, 2025; instead, on October 27-29, 2025, Intellia paused enrollment in the MAGNITUDE and MAGNITUDE-2 trials due to a Grade 4 liver enzyme elevation in one participant, leading to an FDA clinical hold.[2]
indication: Transthyretin (ATTR) amyloidosis with cardiomyopathy (ATTR-CM) and polyneuropathy (ATTR-PN)
pivotal_evidence: MAGNITUDE and MAGNITUDE-2 are Phase 3 registrational trials designed to evaluate efficacy and safety for potential FDA approval of the first CRISPR-based one-time treatment for ATTR amyloidosis[1][3]
primary_endpoint_result: No primary endpoint results were announced; instead, enrollment was paused on October 27, 2025, after one participant experienced Grade 4 liver transaminases and elevated bilirubin on September 30, 2025, triggering protocol-defined safety criteria and an FDA clinical hold on October 29, 2025[1][3][4]
ct_official_title: MAGNITUDE-2: A Phase 3, Multinational, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of NTLA-2001 in Participants With Hereditary Transthyretin Amyloidosis With Polyneuropathy (ATTRv-PN)
ct_conditions: Neuromuscular Disease; Neuromuscular Diseases (NMD); Neurodegenerative Disease; Neurodegenerative Disease, Hereditary; Neurodegenerative Diseases; Neuromuscular Diseases; Nerve Disorders; Nervous System Disease; Nervous System Diseases; Genetic Disease, Inborn; Amyloidosis, Familial; Amyloidosis, Hereditary; Amyloidosis; Polyneuropathies; Amyloid Neuropathies; Amyloid Neuropathies, Familial; Peripheral Nervous System Disease; Peripheral Nervous System Diseases; Metabolism, Inborn Errors; Metabolic Diseases

catalyst_summary: CytomX Therapeutics announced positive interim Phase 1 clinical trial data for CX-2051 in advanced colorectal cancer on or around May 2024, showing a 28% confirmed ORR, 94% disease control rate, and 5.8 months median PFS in 18 efficacy-evaluable patients.[1][2]
primary_endpoint_result: Phase 1 trial primarily assesses safety and tolerability with no DLTs observed and manageable adverse events; encouraging efficacy showed 28% confirmed ORR and 5.8 months median PFS in 18 efficacy-evaluable patients.
ct_official_title: An Investigational Study of CX-2051 in Participants With Advanced Solid Tumors

catalyst_summary: BioAge Labs announced the discontinuation of the STRIDES Phase 2 clinical trial of azelaprag on December 6, 2024, due to observed liver transaminitis in some patients receiving the drug.
primary_endpoint_result: Trial discontinued due to liver transaminitis observed in 11 of 204 patients receiving azelaprag, prior to topline efficacy readout.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel-Arm Proof-of-Concept Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of Oral Azelaprag Plus Once Weekly Tirzepatide Compared with Tirzepatide Alone in Participants with Obesity Aged 55 Years and Over (STRIDES)

catalyst_summary: Janux announced Phase 1 interim clinical data on March 1, 2024, showing JANX007 achieved 73% PSA50 response rate and 26% PSA90 response rate in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer patients, with a manageable safety profile.
primary_endpoint_result: As of February 12, 2024, JANX007 achieved 100% PSA50 response rate and 63% PSA90 response rate in 16 heavily pre-treated mCRPC patients, with 50% of RECIST-evaluable patients showing confirmed or unconfirmed partial responses[6]
ct_official_title: A Phase 1, Open-Label, Multicenter Study of JANX007 in Subjects With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer
ct_conditions: Prostate Cancer; Metastatic Castration-resistant Prostate Cancer; Castration Resistant Prostatic Cancer

catalyst_summary: Immuneering Corp. (IMRX) announced positive updated survival and safety data from its ongoing Phase 2a trial of Atebimetinib combined with modified gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel in first-line pancreatic cancer patients around early October 2024, showing 86% overall survival and 53% progression-free survival at 9 months median follow-up.[1][4]
primary_endpoint_result: Trial showed encouraging survival rates including 86% OS and 53% PFS at 9 months median follow-up, and 94% OS and 72% PFS at 6 months, with median OS and PFS not reached, supporting plans for a future pivotal Phase 3 trial.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2a, Open-Label, Multicenter, Nonrandomized, Safety and Anti-tumor Activity Study of IMM-1-104, a Novel Oral Dual MEK1/2 Inhibitor in Participants With Advanced or Metastatic Solid Tumors
ct_conditions: Advanced Solid Tumor; Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma; Malignant Melanoma (Cutaneous); Non-small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC)

catalyst_summary: Sarepta Therapeutics (SRPT) announced positive topline three-year EMBARK clinical trial results for ELEVIDYS on or around July 23, 2025, showing statistically significant and durable efficacy in slowing DMD motor function decline by ≥70% versus controls with no new safety signals.[1]
pivotal_evidence: EMBARK is a global, randomized placebo-controlled Phase 3 study that served as the pivotal trial supporting ELEVIDYS full approval.
primary_endpoint_result: Missed primary endpoint of improvement in NSAA versus placebo at one year, but showed statistically significant slowing of disease progression (≥70%) on key functional measures versus external controls at three years.
ct_official_title: A Phase 3, Multinational, Long-term Follow-up Study to Evaluate Safety and Efficacy in Subjects Who Have Previously Received SRP-9001 in a Clinical Study

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for SNSE were announced on or around 2024-11-01; the closest updates were in Q3 2024 financial results on November 14 mentioning enrollment progress, with preliminary efficacy data from the Phase 1/2 dose expansion reported later in full year 2024 results.
indication: PD-(L)1 resistant advanced solid tumors (hot tumors including Merkel Cell Carcinoma, NSCLC, Esophageal Cancer, MSI-H CRC)
primary_endpoint_result: 6-month PFS of 50% at 15 mg/kg dose in PD-(L)1 resistant patients and 14% ORR, with responses including a durable complete response in Merkel Cell Carcinoma and partial responses in NSCLC and Esophageal Cancer.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2, Open-label Study Evaluating the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, Pharmacodynamics, and Efficacy of SNS-101 (Anti VISTA) as Monotherapy and in Combination With Cemiplimab in Patients With Advanced Solid Tumors
ct_conditions: Solid Tumor, Adult; Advanced Solid Tumor; Head and Neck Cancer; Breast Cancer; Colon Cancer; Pancreatic Cancer; Gastric Cancer; Esophageal Cancer; Prostate Cancer; Uterine Cancer; Cervix Cancer; Ovarian Cancer; Kidney Cancer; Bladder Cancer; Thyroid Cancer; Melanoma; Sarcoma; Advanced Cancer; Metastatic Cancer; Refractory Cancer; Non Small Cell Lung Cancer; Merkel Cell Carcinoma

catalyst_summary: ImmunityBio (IBRX) announced positive clinical trial results for ANKTIVA plus checkpoint inhibitors in NSCLC from QUILT-2.023 and QUILT-3.055 studies, showing statistically significant immune restoration and improved survival in 151 patients, reported on a Tuesday around early 2026 but referenced in news extending rally on strong results[1][2][5].
primary_endpoint_result: Early Phase 2 data from QUILT-3.078 showed median overall survival not yet reached in 23 patients with 19 alive, statistically significant increases in absolute lymphocyte count from baseline through 20 weeks, and 100% disease control rate in initial 5 patients including 2 near-complete responses[1][3][8].
ct_official_title: Open-Label, Single-Arm Phase 2 Study of Nogapendekin Alfa Inbakicept, PD-L1 t-haNK, Bevacizumab and Randomized Phase 2B Study of Nogapendekin Alfa Inbakicept, Bevacizumab, and Tumor Treatment Fields With or Without PD-L1 t-haNK in Participants With Recurrent or Progressive Glioblastoma

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for VRCA were announced on or around April 17, 2025; the closest events were YCANTH CHMP feedback on April 7, 2025, and prior VP-315 Phase 2 results from August 2024 with additional analyses in November 2025.
primary_endpoint_result: VP-315 achieved 51% complete histologic clearance rate and 97% overall objective response rate, with 86% overall reduction in tumor size across all treated subjects[1][2]
ct_official_title: A Phase 2, Multicenter, Open-label, Proof-of-concept Study With Safety Run-in to Evaluate the Safety, Pharmacokinetics, and Efficacy of VP-315 in Adult Subjects With Basal Cell Carcinoma
ct_conditions: Basal Cell Carcinoma; Skin Cancer; Cancer of the Skin, Basal Cell; Cancer of the Skin; Carcinoma

catalyst_summary: On July 30, 2025, VYNE announced Phase 2b topline results for Repibresib gel in nonsegmental vitiligo showing the trial did not meet primary endpoints, and on July 2, 2025, provided program updates on VYN202 showing promising early Phase 1b efficacy data in plaque psoriasis despite a clinical hold.
primary_endpoint_result: Trial did not meet primary endpoint of F-VASI50 at Week 24 versus vehicle in the modified intent-to-treat population[1][2].

catalyst_summary: Biohaven announced Phase 2 proof-of-concept study results for BHV-7000 in major depressive disorder on December 24, 2025, which missed the primary MADRS endpoint but showed trends favoring the drug in severe depression subgroups and an acceptable safety profile.[1][5]
primary_endpoint_result: Did not meet primary endpoint of reduction in MADRS score over 6 weeks compared to placebo, but showed trends favoring BHV-7000 in severe depression subgroups.
ct_official_title: A Phase 2/3 Multicenter, Randomization, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of BHV-7000 in Subjects With Refractory Focal Onset Epilepsy

catalyst_summary: RAPT Therapeutics announced promising Phase 2 trial results for tivumecirnon in combination with pembrolizumab in CPI-experienced head and neck squamous cell carcinoma patients, showing a confirmed ORR of 15.6% overall on or around February 27, 2024.[1]
indication: Advanced head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC) in CPI-experienced patients
primary_endpoint_result: The trial met its primary objectives of safety and tolerability, with a confirmed objective response rate of 15.6% in all patients (5/32) and 17.4% in PD-L1+ patients (4/23), demonstrating encouraging clinical activity with a well-tolerated safety profile and no increased immune-related toxicity compared to pembrolizumab alone.[1][3]

catalyst_summary: No clinical trial data or results for EDIT were announced on or around March 12, 2025, based on the available search results.

On July 31, 2024, MacroGenics announced halting the Phase 2 trial of vobra duo for prostate cancer due to fatal adverse events, with prior safety disclosures on May 10, 2024, around the queried date of August 8.
primary_endpoint_result: Trial halted on July 31, 2024, due to fatal adverse events (five patient deaths disclosed May 10, 2024); primary endpoint data (6-month radiographic progression-free survival) was presented at ESMO in September 2024 but trial was discontinued before completion.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1, First-in-Human, Dose Escalation Study of MGD024, a CD123 x CD3 Bispecific DART Molecule, in Patients With Select Relapsed or Refractory Hematologic Malignancies
ct_conditions: Leukemia, Acute Myeloid; Myelodysplastic Syndromes; Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma; Leukemia, B-cell; Leukemia, Hairy Cell; Mastocytosis, Aggressive Systemic; Blastic Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cell Neoplasm; Chronic Myeloid Leukemia

catalyst_summary: Immuneering (IMRX) announced positive Phase 2a clinical trial results for atebimetinib in combination with modified gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel in first-line pancreatic cancer on June 17, 2025, reporting 94% overall survival and 72% progression-free survival at 6 months (data cutoff May 26, 2025), not on or around July 1, 2025.
primary_endpoint_result: Reported 94% overall survival and 72% progression-free survival at 6 months (data cutoff May 26, 2025) in Phase 2a trial of atebimetinib + modified gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel.
ct_official_title: A Phase 1/2a, Open-Label, Multicenter, Nonrandomized, Safety and Anti-tumor Activity Study of IMM-1-104, a Novel Oral Dual MEK1/2 Inhibitor in Participants With Advanced or Metastatic Solid Tumors
